_comment: VSCode workspace settings for cOde(n), bundled with the desktop app. Differs from the dev repo's .vscode/settings.json in that python.defaultInterpreterPath is the system 'python' (whatever is on PATH) instead of '.venv/Scripts/python.exe' — the bundled workspace has no .venv/ (the engine runs in a PyInstaller bundle, not a venv). If you don't have Python on PATH, F5 debugging won't work; the cOde(n) Run button still works regardless. To set up Python: install python.org or a distro and ensure 'python' resolves on your shell's PATH.
